text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Skills Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the skills methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every `SKILL.md` MUST have frontmatter with `name` (kebab-case), `description` (≤ 300 chars), `allowed-tools` (whitelist), `trigger-keywords` (≥ 2 phrases).
rule.rationale: Trigger keywords are what makes the skill auto-discoverable; without them the skill only fires on explicit `/<name>` invocation.

rule.statement: `allowed-tools` MUST be the minimum set the workflow actually needs. Defaulting to all tools is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Skills with broad tool access amplify accident blast radius; minimum-scope is the policy default.

rule.statement: Skills shared across projects MUST live in `faion-network/skills/`. Project-specific skills MUST live in the project repo + be gitignored unless the team owns its own skills folder.
rule.rationale: Mixing global + project skills bloats faion-network and creates merge conflicts; clear separation keeps each scope clean.

rule.statement: Detail beyond SKILL.md's body MUST live in `reference.md` or `.agents/<topic>.md`. SKILL.md body stays under 200 lines.
rule.rationale: Auto-loaded SKILL.md burns context budget on every trigger; on-demand `reference.md` is loaded only when the agent calls Read.

rule.statement: Trigger keywords MUST be tested: send 10 user phrasings, confirm the skill auto-fires for the intended ones AND does NOT fire for adjacent skills.
rule.rationale: Untested keywords either miss real users (low recall) or steal calls from other skills (low precision).
